title: allium canadense subsp. fraseri
intro: Allium canadense subsp. fraseri, also known as the Canadian wild garlic or Fraser's wild garlic, is a subspecies of the Allium canadense plant, a type of wild garlic native to North America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to its sulfur compounds. The health benefits of consuming Allium canadense subsp. fraseri include reducing oxidative stress, lowering blood pressure, and supporting immune function. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to leverage its medicinal and health benefits.
